facade_description: 188 Augusta Ave presents a converted house-form, semi-detached facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 1 storeys and is capped by a flat, consistent with its converted house-form, semi-detached typology. The facade reads primarily as painted masonry/stucco in a white/cream painted facade with blue sign and grey trim palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 4 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 1 principal entrance. Character-defining facade elements include cornice. HCD note: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through ground floor alterations. Through its construction date and typology, each property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s
